mnemonics: Use the acronym 'SCARF IS' to remember: Self-describing nature, Concurrency control, Abstraction, Real-world view, Flexibility, Insulation between programs and data, Sharing of data and multiuser transaction processing
keyPoints: Self-describing nature of a database system
Insulation between programs and data (program-data independence)
Support of multiple views of the data
Sharing of data and multiuser transaction processing
Control of data redundancy and enforcement of integrity constraints
commonMistakes: Thinking a DBMS is the same as a database (the DBMS is the software, the database is the data)
Believing that all data redundancy is eliminated (some redundancy might remain for practical reasons)
